---
title: "PyDeepCausalML"
subtitle: "Deep Learning for Causal Inference in Python"
page-layout: full
toc: false
---

::: {.pkg-hero}



# pydeepcausalml {.pkg-name}

**Latest release:** 0.2.0 · **Requires:** Python ≥ 3.9 · **License:** [MIT](https://github.com/zia207/PyDeepCausalML/blob/main/LICENSE)

Deep learning models for causal inference in Python with Pytorch: treatment-effect estimation, causal structure learning, temporal causal discovery, and counterfactual forecasting — behind one consistent, sklearn-style API.

```{.bash copy="true"}
pip install pydeepcausalml
```

| | |
|---|---|
| **Package** | [PyPI](https://pypi.org/project/pydeepcausalml/) · [GitHub](https://github.com/zia207/PyDeepCausalML) |
| **Docs** | [Tutorial](Tutorial/02_08_05_05_00_DeepCausalML_introduction.ipynb) · [Glossary](Tutorial/00-scientific-terminology-glossary.qmd) |
| **Author** | Zia U. Ahmed · Upatta Analytics |

:::

::: {.grid}

::: {.g-col-8}

## What is it? {#what-is-it}

**PyDeepCausalML** consolidates deep causal models from the *Deep Causal ML* tutorial series into a tested, installable Python package. 

Use it when you need to:

- Estimate **average or individual treatment effects** from observational data
- Discover **causal graphs** from static or time-series data
- Run **counterfactual** and **interventional** queries with neural structural models
- Train on **CPU, CUDA, or Apple MPS** with the same sklearn-style API

## Installation {#installation}

```bash
pip install pydeepcausalml            # core
pip install "pydeepcausalml[plot]"    # + matplotlib/seaborn plotting helpers
pip install "pydeepcausalml[dev]"     # + test and lint tooling
```

From source:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/zia207/PyDeepCausalML && cd PyDeepCausalML
pip install -e ".[dev]"
pytest
```

## Main features {#main-features}

- **Treatment effects:** `TARNet`, `CFRNet`, `DragonNet`, `NeuralDML`, `GANITE`, `CEVAE`
- **Generative causal models:** `CausalVAE`, `IVAE`, `CausalDiscrepancyVAE`, `CausalGAN`, `DSCM`, `CausalEGM`
- **Structure learning:** `NOTEARS`, `DAGGNN`, `DagmaLinear`, `GranDag`, `CASTLE`, `DynoTEARS`
- **Time series:** neural Granger (`cMLP`, `cLSTM`), `TCDF`, `CausalTransformer`, `GVAR`, `CausalGNN`
- **Counterfactuals:** `DeepSynth`, `CRN`, `GNet`, `CausalLSTMForecaster`, `DeepSCM`, `DECI`
- **Utilities:** synthetic datasets with ground truth, PEHE/SHD metrics, causal graph plotting

## Quickstart {#quickstart}

```python
from pydeepcausalml import DragonNet, NeuralDML
from pydeepcausalml.datasets import make_confounded_data
from pydeepcausalml.metrics import pehe

df = make_confounded_data(n=5000, random_state=0)
X = df[["age", "education", "prior_income"]].values
t, y = df["treatment"].values, df["outcome"].values

est = DragonNet(epochs=200, random_state=0).fit(X, t, y)
print("ATE:", est.predict_ate(X))
print("PEHE:", pehe(df["true_cate"], est.predict_cate(X)))

dml = NeuralDML(n_splits=2, random_state=0).fit(X, t, y)
print("DML ATE:", dml.predict_ate(), "95% CI:", dml.confidence_interval())
```

## Documentation {#documentation}

The sidebar links to the full **Deep Causal ML** tutorial series — 27 notebooks covering treatment effects, generative latent-variable models, structure learning, and temporal causal ML.

Start here:

- [Introduction to Deep Causal Learning](Tutorial/02_08_05_05_00_DeepCausalML_introduction.ipynb)
- [Treatment Effect Estimators](Tutorial/02_08_05_05_01_00_DeepCausalML_treatment_effect_estimators_introduction.ipynb)
- [Causal Structural Learning](Tutorial/02_08_05_05_03_00_DeepCausalML_structural_learning_introduction.ipynb)
- [Temporal Deep Causal ML](Tutorial/02_08_05_05_04_00_DeepCausalML_timeseries_introduction.ipynb)
- [Scientific Terminology Glossary](Tutorial/00-scientific-terminology-glossary.qmd)

## License {#license}

MIT — see [LICENSE](https://github.com/zia207/PyDeepCausalML/blob/main/LICENSE).

:::

::: {.g-col-4 .pkg-sidebar}

### Navigation

- [Project description](#what-is-it)
- [Installation](#installation)
- [Main features](#main-features)
- [Quickstart](#quickstart)
- [Documentation](#documentation)
- [License](#license)

### Project links

- [Homepage](https://zia207.github.io/PyDeepCausalML/)
- [Repository](https://github.com/zia207/PyDeepCausalML)
- [Issues](https://github.com/zia207/PyDeepCausalML/issues)
- [PyPI](https://pypi.org/project/pydeepcausalml/)

### Verified details

| | |
|---|---|
| **License** | MIT |
| **Author** | Zia U. Ahmed |
| **Requires** | Python ≥ 3.9 |
| **Framework** | PyTorch ≥ 2.0 |

### Optional extras

`plot`, `dev`

### Classifiers

- Development Status :: Beta
- Intended Audience :: Science/Research
- Topic :: Scientific/Engineering :: AI

:::

:::

<style>
.pkg-hero {
  background: linear-gradient(135deg, #f8f9fa 0%, #e9ecef 100%);
  border: 1px solid #dee2e6;
  border-radius: 0.5rem;
  padding: 1.5rem 2rem;
  margin-bottom: 2rem;
}
.pkg-hero .pkg-name {
  margin-top: 0;
  font-size: 2rem;
}
.pkg-hero pre {
  margin-top: 1rem;
  margin-bottom: 0.5rem;
  max-width: 28rem;
}
.pkg-sidebar {
  background: #f8f9fa;
  border: 1px solid #dee2e6;
  border-radius: 0.5rem;
  padding: 1rem 1.25rem;
  font-size: 0.95rem;
}
.pkg-sidebar h3 {
  font-size: 1rem;
  margin-top: 1.25rem;
  margin-bottom: 0.5rem;
  text-transform: uppercase;
  letter-spacing: 0.03em;
  color: #495057;
}
.pkg-sidebar h3:first-child {
  margin-top: 0;
}
</style>
